calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Final Discharge
Off Calendar Aug-01-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Thomson in Propria Persona.

Mr. Thomson stated that he realized that Keelan was a minor when he was in the process of filing the receipts and when the Court pointed it out, and stated that the will provides ablosutley nothing with regard to the $20,000.00 distribution. The Court stated that the $20,000.00 should have gone into a blocked account and the Court could have determined if the funds were to be used for the Minor's schooling. The Court will discharge Mr. Thomson in all regards except relative to the Minor and also discharge bond. When the Minor turns 18 years of age he can decide if he wants to take any action against Mr. Thomson in his capacity as Executor.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-03-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Jonas for Bernard Walp. Edward Napier in Propria Persona. Anthony Critchlow for National Alzheimers Association.

The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions. The Court granted the petition, (signing the order for the same in open court).

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-27-2009 Continued To Jun-03-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-21-2009 Continued To May-27-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Jonah for Bernard Walt. Edward Napier in Propria Persona. Anthony Critchow for the National Alzheimer's Association.

Mr. Critchow stated that he objected to the final distribution and requested a continuance to file an interpretation of the will and his written objection. The matter was continued to May 27, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The written objection is to be filed no later than May 1, 2009. The Parties will proceed with the sale of the automobile.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-01-2009 Continued To Apr-21-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Jonas for Bernard Walp (Surviving Spouse). Edward Thomson in Propria Persona.

Mr. Thomson presented the endorsed copies of the Amended Petition For Settlement of The First Account and the Notice Of Hearing. Mr. Jonas stated that the amended petition addressed his
Client's concerns and that it would not draw objections from his Client. The Court informed Counsel that there was not sufficient time on the notice. The matter was continued to April 21, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for proper notice and for the hearing on the amended petition.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: T. Jackson.
